hero.eyebrow: // program manager · producer · maker of things
hero.heading: Olivia Krowicka
hero.sub: Great products don't ship by accident. I build the structure and set up the building blocks that make successful product delivery possible, keeping people focused on the work that matters.

about.heading: Building with intention — across disciplines.
paragraphs: I'm a Program Manager who works at the intersection of structured systems and creative work. By day, I build the workflows, tooling, and documentation that help teams ship. By night I design games and write.

My PM work is rooted in lean, native tooling — getting the most out of platforms teams already use without introducing tool sprawl. I prototype in a demo space before proposing org-level changes, because nobody likes a surprise restructure.
When I write, it's about process, art, inspiration, and craft — the things that make work worth doing.

stats.label: Org-level GitHub projects built from scratch
Active projects across PM, game design & docs
GDD template polished to benchmark quality

paragraphs: As a kid, I loved taking things apart. I took apart clocks, a sewing machine, and much to the chagrin of my parents.  I didn't always know how to put them back together, but that wasn't really the point. I wanted to see what was inside, and I wanted to understand how it all worked.

I was born in Poland and grew up steeped in the arts, from music to visual art, creative expression as a way of life. That foundation never left. It lives in how I approach problems, how I think about design, and the something extra I bring to the work that's hard to put a name to.

When I came to Canada, I brought all of that with me, along with a deep appreciation for every opportunity in front of me. I don't take any of it for granted. That shapes how hard I work and how genuinely I show up for the people I work with.
That curiosity never went away. I'm still that person who loves getting into the details, taking problems apart and solving one small part at a time.

I'm endlessly curious about systems, about people, about the space where the two meet. What makes them tick? The psychology of why a big red button is so inviting. I could stop at planning and the clean defined boundaries of a job title, but I'm always thinking about who we’re building things for, and how they will use what we’re building, and whether they will use it.

Everything I make is ultimately for people, so understanding their joys, their frustrations, and everything in between is how I make sure I'm solving the right problems and not just the ones that seem easier.

At the heart of it, I love the work. The logic, the reasoning, the moment something clicks into place. But I'm always working toward something human. I want the things I build to bring joy, to make work feel lighter, to leave people better equipped than when I arrived.
I build around people. I learn how they work, what they need, what they're capable of, and then I build something they can own long after I'm no longer in the room.

And there's nothing quite like watching a tiny seed of an idea become something real that exists in the world. Especially when it happens together, a real collaboration where ideas riff off each other and the thing that comes out the other end is better than any one person could have made alone.

values.title: Stay Curious
values.body: Be a lifelong learner, ask questions, seek answers in unusual places. Get inspired by the world around you. Step outside your comfort zone and make trying new things a habit.

values.title: Know The Rules To Break the Rules
values.body: Breaking a rule you understand is a decision; breaking one you don't know exists is a mistake. Know the boundaries well enough to know exactly what happens when you push past them. Be intentional and proactive, not accidental.

values.title: Lean Into Creative Tension
values.body: When you bring different people with different perspectives into the room and let ideas get genuinely challenged, you stop optimizing what already exists and start discovering what's actually possible. The friction is the point.

values.title: Lead With Integrity
values.body: Do what you say, say what you mean. Take responsibility when you're wrong, give credit where it's due, and never position yourself as the hero of a story that belongs to everyone. Integrity is how you show up every day and what you do, even when no one is watching.

values.title: Embrace Change
values.body: The situation will always change. Embracing change is the ability to respond to what's in front of you, find a path through, and still land somewhere better than where you started. It's turning any setback into a win by understanding what success looks like for everyone in the room and finding a way to get there regardless of the obstacles.

values.title: Be Genuine
values.body: Success means nothing if it only belongs to one person. Being genuine means showing up as yourself, caring about the people around you, and wanting everyone to win. We only truly succeed when we elevate those around us.

projects.title: GitHub PM Architecture
projects.summary: A full org-level PM setup built from scratch — YAML issue templates, three Projects v2 boards with custom fields, and GitHub Actions automation for date tracking. Prototyped in a personal demo space before proposing to a real org.

detail.intro: A full org-level GitHub PM setup, built from scratch in a personal demo space before proposing it to a real organization. The goal was to replace a scattered collection of repo-level projects with a coherent, cross-repo architecture using only native GitHub tooling.

sections.heading: What was built
items: A YAML issue template suite covering Epics, User Stories, Tasks, Bug Reports, and Feature Requests — each with structured fields and consistent labelling conventions.
Three org-level Projects v2 boards: a Roadmap (Epics on a timeline by milestone), a Team Planning board (Kanban with iteration support), and a Bug Tracker.
Custom fields including Start Date, End Date, and a Size field (XS–XL mapped to Fibonacci values 1/2/3/5/8) serving as the estimation proxy.
A GitHub Action (set-dates.yml) that auto-populates Start Date on transition to In Progress and End Date on transition to Done, using the Projects v2 GraphQL API.

sections.heading: Key decisions
items: Label-based filtering over issue types — GitHub issue types weren't rendering reliably in project filters, so label-based filtering is the more dependable workaround.
Org-level projects only — repo-level projects lack cross-repo visibility. Org-level is the correct architecture for any team working across multiple repositories.
Size as estimation proxy — avoids introducing a second field and keeps the estimation process lightweight.

detail.callout: Prototyping in a personal demo space before proposing org changes is how you manage stakeholder risk. Nobody likes a surprise restructure.

projects.title: Project Hub Docs Site
projects.summary: A Jekyll + Just the Docs documentation site covering PM workflows, game design references, and onboarding. Auto-deployed on every push via GitHub Actions. Built entirely with native GitHub tooling.

detail.intro: A Jekyll-based documentation site using the Just the Docs theme, covering PM workflows, game design references, and onboarding material. Deployed automatically on every push to main via GitHub Actions.

sections.heading: Tech stack
items: Jekyll with the Just the Docs v0.10.0 theme via remote_theme
GitHub Actions workflow for automatic build and deployment
Structured navigation with section index files and permalink conventions

sections.heading: Site structure
items: GitHub PM — issue templates, project architecture, workflow automation and GraphQL queries
Game Design — GDD template reference and structure documentation

detail.callout: Built entirely with native GitHub tooling. No third-party hosting, no paid CI/CD service, no CMS — just a repo, a theme, and a workflow file.

contact.heading: Let's work together.
contact.sub:
Whether it's a service, software, a game you're building, or something you want to bring to life, or just want someone to collaborate with on a problem.

Reach out and let's chat.
